postmortem: The model overweighted the absence of material news in the 10-Q filing itself while ignoring the timing signal: a routine quarterly disclosure filing often triggers portfolio rebalancing or index reconstitution flows, especially around quarter-end dates like April 30th. The prediction also misjudged the +0.23% pre-filing move as noise rather than recognizing it as potential profit-taking before earnings-adjacent volatility, which would have suggested downside risk rather than neutrality. The confidence should have been lower given that routine filings frequently catalyze technical unwinds in high-beta mega-cap stocks like AMZN, not just fundamental surprises.
